description: read_active_session_count takes a lock_guard on the registry mutex before reading; read_static_buildinfo_count and read_static_feature_count read without synchronisation.

findings.rationale: read_active_session_count constructs a lock_guard before the field read; the other two reads run without synchronisation. The lock is load-bearing: SessionRegistry::active_count is written concurrently by handler threads, and an unsynchronised read is undefined behaviour under the C++ memory model regardless of whether the read happens to look atomic on a given platform.
